text: Walking through these dimly lit, modern corridors, I am struck by the emptiness and silence. There’s an air of anticipation—an environment where human presence is merely implied by design, yet presently absent. Upon entering the next space with its bright and textured wall, I feel a tension between creativity and surveillance, as if the research and technology on display are both inviting me to participate and warning me of their ever-watchful gaze. I find myself pondering the paradox of feeling seen and unseen, of being both participant and observer.
